nodes.note: Live-voice ENTRY (PE-T3). Fires once per finalized caller utterance on a 1:1 support call. The host segments the inbound audio, persists it as a CTB file id (audio/l16), and starts this flow CHATLESS with $json = { event:'utteranceFinal', target, mode:'support', speakerId, audioFileId, audioMime, audioSampleRate }.

STT leg (PB-T7 reused!). source:'file' reads the host-persisted utterance bytes via ctx.files.read — the SAME node the chat voice demo used, now fed by the call event's audioFileId instead of a Telegram file_id. The text lands at $json.transcript.text.
ai:model PROVIDER (PB-T5): which model + credential the agent calls. Reused verbatim — Phase E adds NO AI nodes.
ai:memory PROVIDER (PB-T4): rolling conversation memory keyed by the CALL target (not a chat id — a live call has no chat). The agent replays prior turns of this call before answering.

params.description: Use this scratchpad to reason step-by-step before answering the caller.

nodes.note: ai:tool PROVIDER (PB-T6): the Think reasoning scratchpad.

params.description: Look up the live status of a customer order by its id.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The order id to look up

nodes.note: ai:tool PROVIDER (PB-T6): an HTTP tool the model can call to fetch a live order status.

params.system_prompt: You are a friendly Persian voice-support agent on a live phone call. Answer the caller concisely and naturally for speech. Use the order_status tool when they ask about an order, and the think tool to plan multi-step answers.

nodes.note: The orchestrator (PB-T5): turns the transcribed question into a reply at $json.ai.reply, using the model/memory/tool providers via its typed slots.

TTS leg (PB-T7 reused!). Synthesizes the agent's reply to audio via ctx.ai.speech, stores the bytes via ctx.files.write, and surfaces a CTB file id at $json.speech.fileId. format:'pcm' suits playing straight into the call.

Live-voice ACTION (PE-T4). Plays the synthesized reply INTO the call via ctx.call.speak — source:'file' so the HOST reads the stored bytes (the node never touches raw audio, I6). This closes the real-time voice loop: the caller hears the AI answer back.
